reasoning:
Verdict: REVIEW (10% confidence)

Module Scores:
  Sentinel (CWE/OWASP Web Application Security): 50/100 [Info risk] — 1 info
  Watchdog (OWASP LLM Top 10 / Cisco AI Threat Taxonomy): 100/100 [Info risk] — 1 info
  guardian (unknown): 0/100 [Critical risk] — no findings

Arbitration Triggers:
  • REVIEW trigger — score below 60: Sentinel (50/100)

Disagreement Resolution:
  ⚠ Watchdog scored 100/100 (info risk) while Sentinel scored 50/100 (info risk) — Δ50 point divergence. Arbitration defers to the stricter assessment from Sentinel to maintain safety margins.
  ⚠ Sentinel scored 50/100 (info risk) while guardian scored 0/100 (critical risk) — Δ50 point divergence. Arbitration defers to the stricter assessment from guardian to maintain safety margins.
  ⚠ Watchdog scored 100/100 (info risk) while guardian scored 0/100 (critical risk) — Δ100 point divergence. Arbitration defers to the stricter assessment from guardian to maintain safety margins.

Confidence Factors:
  • Base: 1/2 module(s) independently support REVIEW — 45% baseline conviction
  • -30% confidence: 3 disagreement(s) between modules
  • -15% confidence: 1 module(s) failed to complete — reduced coverage
  • Average module score across 2 completed module(s): 75/100 (reported for context — not directly used in confidence calibration)

--- LLM-Enhanced Council Narrative ---
The overall risk posture of the VeriMedia AI application cannot be meaningfully determined due to critical gaps in the assessment process. Both sentinel and guardian were unable to conduct substantive evaluations—sentinel due to the absence of source code or configuration files (sentinel-1-a5e7b3f9), and guardian due to a failed assessment with no findings or recommendations. This alignment highlights a shared inability to verify security controls or compliance measures, representing a significant risk blind spot.

Watchdog, in contrast, claims to have thoroughly reviewed the application profile and codebase, finding no LLM or generative AI integrations (watchdog-1). However, this directly conflicts with sentinel's report of insufficient data for any code review, raising questions about the scope and completeness of the information provided to each module. Additionally, none of the modules—especially guardian—addressed governance, compliance, or organizational risk management, leaving a critical oversight gap. Until complete and consistent data is made available for all modules, the application's true security and compliance posture remains unknown.

sentinel.summary: No source code or configuration files were available for VeriMedia AI, a Flask-based AI media verification platform. As a result, no security posture assessment can be performed on its Python, Flask, or infrastructure components.
sentinel.recommendation: 1. Provide the full source code and configuration files for VeriMedia AI to enable static security analysis.

top_findings: No LLM or generative AI integrations detected in VeriMedia AI application
watchdog.summary: VeriMedia AI is a media verification platform using deep learning (ResNet, BERT) for image, video, and text analysis. No LLM or generative AI integrations (such as GPT-4, Claude, LangChain, etc.) were detected in the application profile or codebase.
watchdog.recommendation: 1. If planning to integrate LLMs or generative AI, implement input/output validation and prompt boundary controls. 2. Establish monitoring for any future LLM API usage. 3. Reassess security posture if LLMs are introduced.
